chapter_title.en: Chapter 79 – Miscellaneous regarding the state of the Occultation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/18)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/18)

parts.text: عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى

text: أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعِبَادُ مِنَ اللَّهِ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ وَ أَرْضَى مَا يَكُونُ عَنْهُمْ إِذَا افْتَقَدُوا حُجَّةَ اللَّهِ جَلَّ وَ عَزَّ وَ لَمْ يَظْهَرْ لَهُمْ وَ لَمْ يَعْلَمُوا مَكَانَهُ وَ هُمْ فِي ذَلِكَ يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ لَمْ تَبْطُلْ حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ وَ لَا مِيثَاقُهُ فَعِنْدَهَا فَتَوَقَّعُوا الْفَرَجَ صَبَاحاً وَ مَسَاءً

فَإِنَّ أَشَدَّ مَا يَكُونُ غَضَبُ اللَّهِ عَلَى أَعْدَائِهِ إِذَا افْتَقَدُوا حُجَّتَهُ وَ لَمْ يَظْهَرْ لَهُمْ وَ قَدْ عَلِمَ أَنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَهُ لَا يَرْتَابُونَ وَ لَوْ عَلِمَ أَنَّهُمْ يَرْتَابُونَ مَا غَيَّبَ حُجَّتَهُ عَنْهُمْ طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ وَ لَا يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا عَلَى رَأْسِ شِرَارِ النَّاسِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from the one who narrated it, from Al Mufazzal Bin Umar and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Abdullah Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from his father, from one of his companions, from Al Mufazzal Bin Umar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The closest of what the servants can be to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Majestic is His<sup>azwj</sup> Mention, and the Happiest what He<sup>azwj</sup> can be from them is when they would be missing the Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Majestic and Mighty and he<sup>asws</sup> would not be apparent to them and they would not be knowing his<sup>asws</sup> place, and they, during that, would be knowing that it neither invalidates the Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Majestic is His<sup>azwj</sup> Mention, nor His<sup>azwj</sup> Covenant. Thus, during that, they would be anticipating the relief morning and evening.

So the most intensely Wrathful what Allah<sup>azwj</sup> can be upon His<sup>azwj</sup> enemies is when they are missing His<sup>azwj</sup> Divine Authority and he<sup>asws</sup> is not apparent to them, and He<sup>azwj</sup> has Known that His<sup>azwj</sup> friends would not be suspicious, and had He<sup>azwj</sup> Known that they would be suspicious, He<sup>azwj</sup> would not have Caused His<sup>azwj</sup> Divine Authority to be absent from them for the blink of an eye, and that (Wrath) does not happen to be except upon the heads of the most evil of the people’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad ibn Khalid from the person who narrated to him from al-Mufaddal ibn ‘Umar, and Muhammad ibn Yahya from ‘Abdallah ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from his father from certain people of his friends from al-Mufaddal ibn ‘Umar from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “People are nearer to Allah, Majestic is Whose mention, and He is more pleased with them when the leader who possesses authority from Allah, Majestic is Whose mention, will be out of their sight. He would not publicly appear among them and they would not know his place. Despite this they would know that the leader who possesses authority from Allah, Majestic is Whose mention, is not invalidated nor is His covenant as suCh In such a case, they expect relief and happiness through his reappearance in public mornings and evenings. Allah’s anger on His enemies is most intense when the leadership with Allah’s authority would be out of public sight and would not appear to them. He (Allah) knows that his friends do not have doubts. Had it been in His knowledge that they would doubt He would not have caused the leader with His authority to disappear from public sight not even for a blinking of an eye. The reappearance of the Leader with Divine Authority will only take place over the heads of the evil ones among the people.”
